title: Antiprime Numbers
description:
We call a positive integer an antiprime if it has more divisors than any positive integer less than that number. Example antiprime numbers are: 1, 2, 4, 6, 12, 24.

Write a program which:

  • reads from the standard input a positive integer n,
  • computes the largest antiprime number not greater than n,
  • writes the result to the standard output.
input: The first and only line of standard input contains a single integer n, 1 ≤ n ≤ 2,000,000,000.
output: In the only line of standard output your program should write one integer - the largest antiprime number not greater than n.
source: Olympiad, Polish Olympiad in Informatics, POI 2000/2001, Stage 1
